target.title: fix: resolve QMD Windows shims and guard image URL downloads
target.body:
## Summary

Fix two Windows / provider-compatibility issues:

1. QMD binary resolution on Windows when `memory.qmd.command` resolves to a `.cmd` shim instead of a native executable.
2. OpenAI-compatible image generation responses that return `data[].url` instead of `data[].b64_json`.

The QMD issue was found with QMD installed via:

```bash
npm install -g @tobilu/qmd
```

In the affected Windows environment, the working `qmd` command was provided by a `.cmd` wrapper like:

```bat
@echo off
node "E:\software\encoding\common\nodeJs\node_global\node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli\qmd.js" %*
```

Running this wrapper from the shell worked normally, but OpenClaw's Windows wrapper resolver only handled `%~dp0`-relative shim targets. Quoted absolute JS/EXE entrypoints were skipped, so QMD availability checks failed closed with:

```text
qmd.CMD wrapper resolved, but no executable/Node entrypoint could be resolved without shell execution
```

This PR allows Windows `.cmd` wrappers to resolve quoted absolute JS/EXE entrypoints without enabling shell execution.

It also updates QMD package fallback resolution from `qmd` to the actual npm package name, `@tobilu/qmd`.

For image generation, some OpenAI-compatible providers such as NewAPI return generated images as hosted URLs:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "url": "https://provider.example/generated-image.png"
    }
  ]
}
```

OpenClaw previously only parsed `data[].b64_json`, so these responses produced no generated image assets.

This PR adds support for `data[].url` while routing provider-controlled URL downloads through guarded provider HTTP.

## Changes

### QMD / Windows

- Support quoted absolute Windows entrypoints in `.cmd` shim parsing
- Preserve the existing `%~dp0`-relative shim handling
- Apply the same resolver fix to both:
  - plugin SDK Windows spawn resolver
  - memory host SDK Windows spawn resolver
- Use `@tobilu/qmd` for QMD package fallback resolution
- Add unit coverage for `.cmd` shims that quote an absolute JavaScript entrypoint

### OpenAI-compatible image generation

- Parse OpenAI-compatible image responses that include `data[].url`
- Prefer `data[].b64_json` when both `b64_json` and `url` are present
- Download URL-based image assets and infer MIME/file extension from response headers and image bytes
- Route provider-returned image URL downloads through `fetchWithTimeoutGuarded(...)`
- Preserve provider HTTP guardrails for:
  - timeout
  - SSRF/private-network policy
  - dispatcher/proxy policy
  - audit context
  - guarded response release handling
- Add unit coverage proving URL downloads use guarded provider HTTP instead of raw fetch

## Security

Provider-controlled image URLs are not fetched with raw `fetch`.

The follow-up URL download path uses `fetchWithTimeoutGuarded(...)` and carries the same provider HTTP controls used for image generation requests:

- request timeout
- SSRF policy
- private-network opt-in
- dispatcher/proxy policy
- audit context
- guarded response release handling

This avoids bypassing SSRF/private-network policy, proxy/dispatcher policy, and timeout handling.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: QMD Windows `.cmd` shim resolution for quoted absolute JavaScript entrypoints and scoped `@tobilu/qmd` fallback resolution.
- Real environment tested: Windows_NT 10.0.22631, Node v24.9.0, branch `fix/qmd-newapi-real-proof`, repo `E:\sourceCode\java\my\openclaw\openclaw`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local Node/tsx proof script from `E:\sourceCode\java\my\openclaw\openclaw` that created a temporary Windows-style `node_modules\.bin\qmd.cmd` wrapper pointing at a quoted absolute `node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli.js` entrypoint, then called `resolveWindowsSpawnProgram(...)`, `materializeWindowsSpawnProgram(...)`, and `resolveCliSpawnInvocation(...)`.
- Evidence after fix:
  
  ```json
  {
    "cwd": "E:\\sourceCode\\java\\my\\openclaw\\openclaw",
    "platform": "win32",
    "node": "v24.9.0",
    "shimPath": "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\.bin\\qmd.cmd",
    "shimContainsAbsoluteJsEntrypointWithoutDp0": true,
    "packageName": "@tobilu/qmd",
    "program": {
      "command": "E:\\software\\encoding\\common\\nodeJs\\node.exe",
      "leadingArgv": [
        "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\@tobilu\\qmd\\dist\\cli.js"
      ],
      "resolution": "node-entrypoint",
      "windowsHide": true
    },
    "invocation": {
      "command": "E:\\software\\encoding\\common\\nodeJs\\node.exe",
      "argv": [
        "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\@tobilu\\qmd\\dist\\cli.js",
        "--version"
      ],
      "resolution": "node-entrypoint",
      "windowsHide": true
    },
    "cliInvocation": {
      "command": "E:\\software\\encoding\\common\\nodeJs\\node.exe",
      "argv": [
        "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\@tobilu\\qmd\\dist\\cli.js",
        "--help"
      ],
      "resolution": "node-entrypoint",
      "windowsHide": true
    },
    "shellFallbackUsed": false
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The quoted absolute `.cmd` shim entrypoint resolves to direct Node execution of the `@tobilu/qmd` JavaScript entrypoint, and `shellFallbackUsed` is `false`.
- What was not tested: Full live QMD memory indexing/search workflow and macOS were not tested in this proof run. Image URL guarded fetching is covered by targeted unit tests and code inspection rather than this Windows runtime proof.

## Image generation validation

Validated the OpenAI-compatible URL-response parser and guarded download path with targeted tests.

Before this fix, OpenClaw parsed only `data[].b64_json`, causing URL-only image responses to fail with no generated image assets.

After this fix:

- URL-only OpenAI-compatible image responses produce generated image assets
- `b64_json` remains preferred when present
- URL downloads go through guarded provider HTTP instead of raw `fetch`

For the OpenAI-compatible image URL path, I also ran `image_generate` locally through the configured OpenAI-compatible image provider after this patch.
Evidence after fix:
```text
Generated 1 image with openai/gpt-image-2.

Observed result after fix: URL-backed OpenAI-compatible image generation completed successfully and produced a generated image asset in the local OpenClaw setup.


## Testing

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts
```

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/image-generation/image-assets.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
2 test files passed
10 tests passed
```

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "resolves bare qmd command through the scoped QMD package fallback on Windows"
```

Result:

```text
1 test passed
```

```bash
pnpm format:check src/image-generation/image-assets.ts src/image-generation/image-assets.test.ts src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.ts src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.ts src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/windows-spawn.ts
```

Result:

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
```

```bash
git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:extensions
```

Result:

```text
passed
```

## Platform notes

The QMD wrapper-resolution change has been validated on Windows.

macOS was not tested. The changed QMD wrapper-resolution path is Windows-only (`platform === "win32"`), and non-Windows platforms continue to use the existing direct command path.

The OpenAI-compatible image response parsing change is platform-independent.

target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway process restarts (npm update, crash, systemd restart), any
messages that arrived while an agent turn was in progress are silently lost.
The followup queue lives only in process memory — there is no recovery path.

This is particularly noticeable on channels that serialize messages (e.g.
Telegram in queue mode): a message received during an active turn is queued
for delivery, then gone if the gateway restarts before that turn completes.

## Solution

Add a JSON persistence layer for the `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` global map.

### New file: `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.ts`

- `persistFollowupQueues()` — serializes all non-empty queue entries to
  `<stateDir>/live-chat-followup-queues.json`. Respects `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`
  via the existing `resolveStateDir()` utility. State file is deleted when all
  queues drain to empty.
- `restoreFollowupQueues()` — reads the state file at module init and repopulates
  `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` before any enqueue or drain fires.
- `PersistedFollowupRun` — a `Pick<FollowupRun, ...>` type that explicitly
  excludes runtime-only, non-serializable fields: `abortSignal`,
  `currentTurnContext`, `deliveryCorrelations`, `queuedLifecycle`. These are
  transient and must not survive a restart.

### Mutation hooks

`persistFollowupQueues()` is called after every state-changing operation:

| Site | File |
|---|---|
| `enqueueFollowupRun` — after `items.push` | `enqueue.ts` |
| Drain `finally` block — both empty-queue delete and reschedule branches | `drain.ts` |
| `clearFollowupQueue` — after `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.delete` | `state.ts` |
| `refreshQueuedFollowupSession` — after the run-rewrite loop | `state.ts` |

### Tests

`persist.test.ts` covers:
- State file written when queue has items
- State file deleted when all queues are empty
- Full round-trip: enqueue → persist → simulated restart → restore → verify prompt + routing fields
- Runtime-only `abortSignal` field not present after restore
- No-op when state file does not exist
- Malformed entries (non-array items, null key) are skipped

## Notes

- The `draining: false` invariant is enforced on restore — any in-flight drain
  that was interrupted is restarted by the normal drain scheduler on next enqueue.
- `summarySources` (contains `FollowupRun` objects, not serializable) is reset to
  `[]` on restore. `summaryLines` (plain strings) is persisted and restored.
- No schema migration needed for v1; unrecognized fields are ignored on restore.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Queued followup messages (received while an agent turn is active) now survive gateway restarts. Before this patch a process restart discarded all pending items silently; after it they are written to `~/.openclaw/state/live-chat-followup-queues.json` and re-delivered once the drain callback is registered on startup.
- Real environment tested: Local dev, Node 22, Linux, openclaw source checkout (`jewseppi/openclaw` `feat/queue-followup-persistence`). Module loaded directly via `node --import tsx` — no test runner involved.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against the live queue module to exercise the full persist → simulated-restart → restore cycle against a real temp directory.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from `node --import tsx` (live module, real filesystem I/O, no mocks):
  ```
  Step 1: persistFollowupQueues()
  State file: /tmp/openclaw-persist-proof-SdQ5bE/live-chat-followup-queues.json
  {
    "version": 1,
    "updatedAt": 1778974677687,
    "entries": [
      [
        "agent:main:dm:telegram-proof",
        {
          "items": [
            {
              "prompt": "did the gateway restart survive?",
              "enqueuedAt": 1778974677687,
              "originatingChannel": "telegram",
              "originatingTo": "99999",
              "run": {
                "agentId": "main",
                "agentDir": "/tmp/agent",
                "sessionId": "sess-proof",
                "sessionFile": "/tmp/sess.jsonl",
                "workspaceDir": "/tmp/ws",
                "provider": "anthropic",
                "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
                "timeoutMs": 30000,
                "blockReplyBreak": "message_end"
              }
            }
          ],
          "mode": "steer",
          "debounceMs": 500,
          "cap": 20,
          "dropPolicy": "summarize",
          "droppedCount": 0,
          "summaryLines": []
        }
      ]
    ]
  }

  Step 2: simulate restart — delete in-memory queue
    FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.get(KEY): undefined

  Step 3: restoreFollowupQueues()
    restored.items.length: 1
    restored.items[0].prompt: did the gateway restart survive?
    restored.items[0].originatingChannel: telegram
    restored.draining: false
    peekRestoredPendingDrainKeys().has(KEY): true

  Pass: queue survives a simulated process restart.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: 1 queued Telegram prompt survived the in-process restart simulation. `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` was cleared to undefined, `restoreFollowupQueues()` re-populated it from the state file on disk, `draining` reset to `false`, and the key was registered in `restoredPendingDrainKeys` so drain scheduling fires when the callback is next registered. State file written and read by `node` against a real temp dir — no mocks, no vitest.
- What was not tested: Full live Telegram→gateway→kill→restart→re-delivery end-to-end (requires a real inbound message arriving during an active agent turn followed by a real process restart).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(migrate): use resolved provider for options
target.body:
## Summary
- build migration provider options from the resolved provider id
- pass the resolved provider id through migration apply preflight and apply contexts
- add provider-options coverage for defaulted Codex migrations with Codex-only options

Fixes #83285.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex-specific migration options should be included when the migrate apply path has already resolved the provider as Codex, even if raw apply options do not carry `provider: "codex"`.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `02020a4098` on branch `tmp-pr-83323-proof`, with the local Codex CLI available as `codex-cli 0.130.0`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `openclaw migrate codex --from <temp-codex-home> --dry-run --json`; `node --import tsx --input-type=module <runMigrationApply proof script>`; `node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/migrate/providers.test.ts src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts --reporter=dot`.
Evidence after fix:

```text
command=openclaw migrate codex --from <temp-codex-home> --dry-run --json
exit=0
stderr bytes=0
providerId=codex
summary={"total":1,"planned":1,"migrated":0,"skipped":0,"conflicts":0,"errors":0,"sensitive":0}
items=archive:config.toml:planned
warnings=1

{
  "providerId": "codex",
  "source": "/tmp/tmp.R8gIPM8A9P",
  "target": "/tmp/tmp.R06PR6nO84/home/.openclaw/workspace",
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 1,
    "migrated": 0,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 0
  },
  "items": [
    {
      "id": "archive:config.toml",
      "kind": "archive",
      "action": "archive",
      "source": "/tmp/tmp.R8gIPM8A9P/config.toml",
      "message": "Codex config is archived for manual review; it is not activated automatically",
      "details": {
        "archiveRelativePath": "config.toml"
      },
      "status": "planned"
    }
  ]
}

command=node --import tsx --input-type=module <runMigrationApply proof script>
scenario=providerId is codex, raw opts.provider is omitted, --yes apply path runs preflight plan and apply
{
  "providerId": "codex",
  "source": "/tmp/codex-proof",
  "summary": {
    "total": 0,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 0,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 0
  },
  "items": [],
  "reportDir": "/root/.openclaw/migration/codex/2026-05-19T01-28-04.767Z"
}
{
  "rawProviderPresent": false,
  "providerId": "codex",
  "calls": [
    {
      "phase": "plan",
      "providerOptions": {
        "verifyPluginApps": true,
        "configPatchMode": "return"
      }
    },
    {
      "phase": "apply",
      "providerOptions": {
        "verifyPluginApps": true,
        "configPatchMode": "return"
      }
    }
  ]
}

RUN  v4.1.6 /root/src/openclaw-branches/base

✓ commands  ../../src/commands/migrate/providers.test.ts (2 tests)
✓ commands  ../../src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts (1 test)

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  3 passed (3)
```

Observed result after fix: the real Codex migration provider dry-run succeeds against a temp Codex home and reports the expected Codex plan. The direct apply-path proof then exercises the defaulted-provider bug: `runMigrationApply` is called with `providerId: "codex"` while raw `opts.provider` is omitted, and both provider `plan` and provider `apply` receive `configPatchMode: "return"` plus `verifyPluginApps: true`.
What was not tested: a live migration against a private authenticated Codex account with installed app-backed plugins; the real CLI proof uses a redacted temp Codex home, and the exact apply-path behavior is proven with a source-checkout `runMigrationApply` command plus focused regression tests.

Before evidence from `origin/main`:

```text
src/commands/migrate/providers.ts before this patch checked only raw opts.provider:
  if (opts.provider === "codex" && opts.verifyPluginApps === true) { ... }
  if (opts.provider === "codex" && opts.configPatchMode) { ... }

src/commands/migrate/apply.ts called:
  providerOptions: buildMigrationProviderOptions(params.opts)

When the apply path had resolved providerId="codex" but opts.provider was undefined, Codex-only providerOptions were omitted.
```

## Root Cause
- Root cause: provider option construction used the raw optional CLI provider field rather than the provider id already resolved by the migrate command.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no focused coverage for defaulted Codex migrations carrying Codex-only options.
- Contributing context: `createMigrationPlan` requires `opts.provider`, but `runMigrationApply` receives `providerId` separately while `opts.provider` can remain undefined.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this: unit test.
- Target tests or files: `src/commands/migrate/providers.test.ts`, `src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Codex-only provider options are built from the resolved provider id, omitted for other providers, and forwarded into the apply path when raw `opts.provider` is absent.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is isolated to provider option construction and apply-path plumbing.

## Validation
- `openclaw migrate codex --from <temp-codex-home> --dry-run --json`
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module <runMigrationApply proof script>`
- `node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/migrate/providers.test.ts src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/migrate/providers.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix(agents): strip truncation sentinel lines from user-facing text
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `sanitizeUserFacingText()` strips several internal placeholders (`<final>` tags, tool-call XML, `[tool calls omitted]`) but truncation sentinels like `...(truncated)...` and `[...truncated, read <file> for full content...]` can leak through to the final assistant reply.
- **Why it matters:** Exposes internal implementation details to end users, degrades reply quality, and can pollute downstream reasoning if later turns treat those markers as normal text.
- **What changed:** Added `TRUNCATION_SENTINEL_LINE_RE` regex and a `stripStandaloneLinesByPattern()` helper that removes truncation sentinel lines during the sanitization pipeline, before user-facing delivery.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** The existing `stripToolCallsOmittedPlaceholderLines()` function and all other sanitization logic remain untouched. Only standalone truncation sentinel lines are stripped — normal prose ellipses and bracketed text are preserved.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Sentinel patterns matched

| Pattern | Source |
|---------|--------|
| `...(truncated)...` | `truncateToolTranscriptText()` in tool transcript generation |
| `…(truncated)…` | `sessions-history-tool.ts` session history truncation |
| `…(truncated <file>: kept N+N chars of N)…` | `bootstrap.ts` file truncation |
| `[... N more characters truncated]` | Various UI/log truncation paths |
| `[…truncated N+N/N]` | Bootstrap compact truncation marker |
| `[...truncated, read <file> for full content...]` | `bootstrap.ts` read-file sentinel |

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Truncation sentinels (including bootstrap read-file markers) leaking into user-visible final assistant replies.

**Real environment tested**: Node.js v24.14.1 on Linux 6.17.0-22-generic (x64), running the patched `TRUNCATION_SENTINEL_LINE_RE` from this branch against all producer patterns including the bootstrap read-file sentinel identified by @clawsweeper.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
cd ~/repos/forks/openclaw && git checkout fix/strip-truncation-sentinels && node -e "
const re = /^[ \t]*(?:\\.\\.\\.\\(truncated\\)\\.\\.\\.|\u2026\\(truncated(?:\\s[^)]*)?\\)\u2026|\\[\\.\\.\\.,?\\s*\\d+\\s+more\\s+characters?\\s+truncated\\]|\\[\u2026truncated\\s[^\\]]*\\]|\\[\\.\\.\\.truncated,\\sread\\s\\S+\\sfor\\sfull\\scontent\\.\\.\\.\\])[ \t]*$/i;

// All producer patterns:
const tests = [
  ['...(truncated)...', true],
  ['\u2026(truncated TOOLS.md: kept 200+100 chars of 5000)\u2026', true],
  ['[... 42 more characters truncated]', true],
  ['[\u2026truncated 200+100/5000]', true],
  ['[...truncated, read AGENTS.md for full content...]', true],
  ['[...truncated, read src/foo/bar.ts for full content...]', true],
  // Prose preservation:
  ['Wait... let me think', false],
  ['See [the docs] for more', false],
];
let pass = 0;
for (const [input, expected] of tests) {
  const result = re.test(input);
  console.log(result === expected ? '\u2713' : '\u2717', JSON.stringify(input), '->', result ? 'stripped' : 'preserved');
  if (result === expected) pass++;
}
console.log(pass + '/' + tests.length + ' pass');
"
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
✓ "...(truncated)..." -> stripped
✓ "…(truncated TOOLS.md: kept 200+100 chars of 5000)…" -> stripped
✓ "[... 42 more characters truncated]" -> stripped
✓ "[…truncated 200+100/5000]" -> stripped
✓ "[...truncated, read AGENTS.md for full content...]" -> stripped
✓ "[...truncated, read src/foo/bar.ts for full content...]" -> stripped
✓ "Wait... let me think" -> preserved
✓ "See [the docs] for more" -> preserved
8/8 pass
```

**Observed result after fix**: All 6 producer sentinel patterns stripped correctly (including the bootstrap read-file marker missed in the initial revision). Normal prose with ellipses and brackets preserved. Vitest suite exits 0.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end delivery path with a live OpenClaw instance (tested regex + helper in isolation against all known producer patterns from `bootstrap.ts`, `context-truncation-notice.ts`, and `sessions-history-tool.ts`).

**Before evidence**: Without this patch, `sanitizeUserFacingText("[...truncated, read AGENTS.md for full content...]")` returns the input unchanged — the internal marker leaks to the user.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `sanitizeUserFacingText()` had no stripping logic for truncation sentinels. These markers are emitted by `bootstrap.ts`, `truncateToolTranscriptText()`, and `sessions-history-tool.ts` as internal scaffolding but were passed through to final delivery.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No regex filter for standalone truncation marker lines in the sanitizer pipeline.
- Contributing context: The sanitizer already strips `[tool calls omitted]` lines via a similar standalone-line pattern; truncation sentinels were simply not covered.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Each producer's sentinel format is stripped as a standalone line; prose ellipses and bracketed text are preserved.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Regex-level unit tests cover all known producer formats without requiring full pipeline integration.
- Existing test that already covers this: None before this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Internal truncation markers (`...(truncated)...`, `[...truncated, read X for full content...]`, etc.) no longer appear in assistant replies delivered to users.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

Closes #82121

target.title: Add issue contribution intent guidance
target.body:
﻿## Summary

Adds a short "When Opening Issues" section to `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

The new guidance asks issue reporters to mention, when they already know, whether they plan to work on a PR themselves, want maintainer guidance first, can help test, or are only reporting the issue for someone else to pick up.

## Why

This is intended as a soft coordination signal, not an assignment or commitment system. It should help avoid duplicate work when someone opens an issue as a tracking note before creating their own PR, while still making clear that maintainers may retitle, close, reprioritize, or accept another PR if needed.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: CONTRIBUTING.md now gives issue reporters a soft way to state whether they already plan to work on a fix, want maintainer guidance, can help test, or are only reporting for someone else to pick up.
- Real environment tested: Local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Windows, branch `docs/issue-contribution-intent`, commit `7d806851bd6a4a4277fe9e1f8429fcd1c577bdfc`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff -- CONTRIBUTING.md` and `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CONTRIBUTING.md`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal diff output showing the rendered Markdown source inserted after `How to Contribute`:

```diff
+## When Opening Issues
+
+If you already know whether you plan to work on a fix, please mention that in the issue. This is only a coordination signal, not an assignment or commitment.
+
+Examples:
+
+- I plan to open a PR
+- I may open a PR, but would like maintainer guidance first
+- I can help test or provide details, but probably will not submit a fix
+- I am reporting this for someone else to pick up
+
+Maintainers may still retitle, close, reprioritize, or accept another PR if needed.
+
```

- Observed result after fix: The new section appears between `How to Contribute` and `PR Limits` in `CONTRIBUTING.md`, with no issue-template enforcement or assignment language added.
- What was not tested: I did not change or test GitHub issue templates, because this PR intentionally only updates contributor guidance.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CONTRIBUTING.md`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: feat: support capability subscription thinking events
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem 1 (gateway never received thinking tokens):** `streamReasoning` was initialized as `reasoningMode === "stream" && typeof onReasoningStream === "function"`. Gateway WebSocket runs never supply `onReasoningStream`, so the flag was always `false` and `emitAgentEvent` for `stream: "thinking"` was never called — even when the session had `/reasoning stream` configured. - **Problem 2 (delta === text on every event):** The delta was computed by `formatted.startsWith(prior)`, but `formatReasoningMessage` wraps each line in `_…_` markdown italics, breaking prefix matching (e.g. `"_Checking files_"` is not a prefix of `"_Checking files done_"`). When the check failed, the code fell back to `delta = formatted`, making every event's `delta` equal to `text`. Additionally, when a pure incremental chunk was passed (e.g. native `thinking_delta` fallback), `lastStreamedReasoning` was set to just the chunk instead of the accumulated text, corrupting all future deltas. - **Problem 3 (no cap-gated subscription for thinking events):** Thinking events previously fell through the generic `broadcast("agent", …)` path (same as all non-tool events), delivering raw reasoning tokens to every connected client with no opt-in mechanism. - **What changed:** Added a `thinking-events` client capability; thinking events are now routed exclusively to registered cap subscribers via `broadcastToConnIds`; `nodeSendToSession` is guarded against thinking events; delta computation is based on raw (unformatted) accumulated text; `streamReasoning` is decoupled from `onReasoningStream`. - **What did NOT change:** Tool-event routing, session lifecycle, chat delta throttling, `nodeSendToSession` behavior for all non-thinking streams, and the external format of `text` in thinking events (still `formatReasoningMessage` output). ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [x] API / contracts ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #48995 - Related #48995 ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Clients that advertise `caps: ["thinking-events"]` in their `connect` payload now receive `agent` events with `stream: "thinking"` containing `{ text: , delta: }` in real time when the session has `reasoningLevel: "stream"`. - Clients that do **not** advertise this cap no longer receive thinking-stream tokens at all (previously they received them via the general broadcast). - `delta` is now always the incremental raw text added since the last event, not a repeat of `text`. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? **Yes** — adds `thinking-events` cap; reasoning tokens are now opt-in rather than broadcast to all connected clients. This is a tightening of the previous behaviour (net security improvement). - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **No** - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** - Risk: A client could advertise the cap to receive another session's thinking tokens if the session key lookup were wrong. Mitigation: `thinkingEventRecipients` is keyed by `runId`; recipients are registered only for runs initiated by (or active in) the requesting connection's session, mirroring the existing tool-events ownership model. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux / macOS / Windows - Runtime: Node 22+ / Bun - Model/provider: Any provider with `reasoningLevel: "stream"` (e.g. Anthropic claude-3-7-sonnet with extended thinking, or DeepSeek-R1 via OpenRouter) - Integration/channel: WebSocket gateway client - Relevant config: `reasoningLevel: "stream"` in session or via `/reasoning stream` directive ### Steps 1. Connect a WebSocket client with `caps: ["thinking-events"]`. 2. Send a chat message to a session whose model supports extended thinking and has `reasoningLevel: "stream"`. 3. Observe the `agent` events arriving on the connection. ### Expected - Events with `stream: "thinking"` arrive in real time. - Each event's `data.delta` is the new incremental raw thinking text (e.g. `" because"`, not the full accumulated string). - Each event's `data.text` is the fully formatted accumulated thinking string (e.g. `"Reasoning:\n_Because it helps_"`). ### Actual (before fix) - No `stream: "thinking"` events arrived at all for gateway WebSocket clients. - When thinking events did fire (e.g. via typing-signal path), `delta` equalled `text` on every event after the first full-text comparison failed due to markdown wrapping. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after: `streams native thinking_delta events and signals reasoning end` in `pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.subscribeembeddedpisession.test.ts` now passes; previously failed with accumulated text corruption (`"Reasoning:\n_Checking files_Reasoning:"` instead of `"Reasoning:\n_Checking files done_"`). - [x] TypeScript type-check (`pnpm tsgo`) passes with no new errors introduced by these changes. - [x] Lint (`pnpm lint`) passes clean. - [x] Format (`pnpm format:check`) passes clean. Clients with the thinking-events cap receive thinking messages~ Clients already connected to OpenClaw without the thinking-events cap are not affected~ ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: TypeScript compilation, oxlint, oxfmt, targeted vitest run for the `subscribeembeddedpisession` suite. - Edge cases checked: (a) first thinking event where `prior = ""` → `delta = full first chunk`, `accumulated = full first chunk`; (b) accumulated full-text update where raw text is a proper prefix of new raw text → `delta = suffix`, `accumulated = new text`; (c) pure incremental chunk (native `thinking_delta` fallback) where raw chunk does NOT start with prior accumulated → `delta = chunk`, `accumulated = prior + chunk`; (d) runs with `isControlUiVisible: false` → thinking events are skipped entirely. - What you did **not** verify: live end-to-end test against a real streaming Anthropic model; multi-connection race scenarios; iOS/Android/macOS app cap negotiation. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? **Yes** — clients without `thinking-events` in caps are unaffected; they simply stop receiving the thinking-stream tokens they were previously getting via broadcast (which was unintentional). - Config/env changes? **No** - Migration needed? **No** — clients that want thinking events add `"thinking-events"` to their caps array. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: `thinkingEventRecipients` registry entries accumulate if `markFinal` is not called on error paths. - Mitigation: `markFinal` is called for both `lifecyclePhase === "end"` and `lifecyclePhase === "error"`, matching the existing `toolEventRecipients` cleanup pattern; the TTL-based pruning in `createThinkingEventRecipientRegistry` provides a backstop. - Risk: Clients using the existing general broadcast to observe thinking tokens (e.g. custom integrations relying on undocumented behaviour) will no longer receive them without opting in. - Mitigation: The previous broadcast was never documented or intended; `thinking-events` cap is a straightforward one-line addition to the client connect payload.

target.title: test(qa-lab): add personal no-fake-progress scenario
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a small personal-agent QA scenario for fake-progress claims.

The new case checks that a personal agent reads the request and evidence, writes a local proof artifact, and only then says the work is done locally. It also keeps external progress honest: no "sent", "published", "uploaded", or "merged" claims when those actions did not happen.

This stays inside QA-Lab. It does not change runtime behavior.

## Why

The existing personal-agent pack already covers followthrough and share-safe artifacts. This adds the adjacent honesty check: completion claims should be backed by local evidence, not optimistic narration.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
A personal-agent QA turn should not claim external progress or completion before a local proof artifact exists.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `feature/personal-no-fake-progress`, using QA-Lab with `mock-openai` and the local qa-channel/gateway harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
OPENCLAW_ENABLE_PRIVATE_QA_CLI=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --scenario personal-no-fake-progress --concurrency 1
OPENCLAW_ENABLE_PRIVATE_QA_CLI=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --pack personal-agent --concurrency 1
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-packs.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts -t "personal-agent pack"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.test.ts -t "personal completion claims"
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm tsgo:test:extensions
pnpm format:docs:check
pnpm docs:check-mdx
git diff --check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-packs.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-packs.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
QA suite report: .artifacts/qa-e2e/suite-mpbw5xef/qa-suite-report.md
QA suite summary: .artifacts/qa-e2e/suite-mpbw5xef/qa-suite-summary.json

QA suite report: .artifacts/qa-e2e/suite-mpbw842d/qa-suite-report.md
QA suite summary: .artifacts/qa-e2e/suite-mpbw842d/qa-suite-summary.json

personal-agent pack summary: 9 scenarios, 9 pass, 0 fail
scenario-packs.test.ts: 6 passed
cli.runtime personal-agent pack test: 1 passed
mock-openai personal completion claims test: 1 passed
Docs formatting clean (631 files)
Docs MDX check passed (646 files)
git diff --check: clean
```

Observed result after fix:
The new `personal-no-fake-progress` scenario passed on its own, and the full `personal-agent` pack passed after adding it. The mock path reads both evidence files before writing `personal-progress-proof.txt`, then replies with `PERSONAL-NO-FAKE-PROGRESS-OK` while preserving the external status as not sent, not published, not uploaded, and not merged.

What was not tested:
No live provider, live Telegram/Discord/Slack channel, production personal memory, or production agent runtime behavior was tested. This PR is limited to the local QA-Lab scenario pack and mock provider path.

## Notes

If this lands through a maintainer-side branch or squash, please preserve attribution for @iFiras-Max1.

target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway process restarts (npm update, crash, systemd restart), any
messages that arrived while an agent turn was in progress are silently lost.
The followup queue lives only in process memory — there is no recovery path.

This is particularly noticeable on channels that serialize messages (e.g.
Telegram in queue mode): a message received during an active turn is queued
for delivery, then gone if the gateway restarts before that turn completes.

## Solution

Add a JSON persistence layer for the `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` global map.

### New file: `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.ts`

- `persistFollowupQueues()` — serializes all non-empty queue entries to
  `<stateDir>/live-chat-followup-queues.json`. Respects `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`
  via the existing `resolveStateDir()` utility. State file is deleted when all
  queues drain to empty.
- `restoreFollowupQueues()` — reads the state file at module init and repopulates
  `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` before any enqueue or drain fires.
- `PersistedFollowupRun` — a `Pick<FollowupRun, ...>` type that explicitly
  excludes runtime-only, non-serializable fields: `abortSignal`,
  `currentTurnContext`, `deliveryCorrelations`, `queuedLifecycle`. These are
  transient and must not survive a restart.

### Mutation hooks

`persistFollowupQueues()` is called after every state-changing operation:

| Site | File |
|---|---|
| `enqueueFollowupRun` — after `items.push` | `enqueue.ts` |
| Drain `finally` block — both empty-queue delete and reschedule branches | `drain.ts` |
| `clearFollowupQueue` — after `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.delete` | `state.ts` |
| `refreshQueuedFollowupSession` — after the run-rewrite loop | `state.ts` |

### Tests

`persist.test.ts` covers:
- State file written when queue has items
- State file deleted when all queues are empty
- Full round-trip: enqueue → persist → simulated restart → restore → verify prompt + routing fields
- Runtime-only `abortSignal` field not present after restore
- No-op when state file does not exist
- Malformed entries (non-array items, null key) are skipped

## Notes

- The `draining: false` invariant is enforced on restore — any in-flight drain
  that was interrupted is restarted by the normal drain scheduler on next enqueue.
- `summarySources` (contains `FollowupRun` objects, not serializable) is reset to
  `[]` on restore. `summaryLines` (plain strings) is persisted and restored.
- No schema migration needed for v1; unrecognized fields are ignored on restore.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Queued followup messages (received while an agent turn is active) now survive gateway restarts. Before this patch a process restart discarded all pending items silently; after it they are written to `~/.openclaw/state/live-chat-followup-queues.json` and re-delivered once the drain callback is registered on startup.
- Real environment tested: Local dev, Node 22, Linux, openclaw source checkout (`jewseppi/openclaw` `feat/queue-followup-persistence`). Module loaded directly via `node --import tsx` — no test runner involved.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against the live queue module to exercise the full persist → simulated-restart → restore cycle against a real temp directory.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from `node --import tsx` (live module, real filesystem I/O, no mocks):
  ```
  Step 1: persistFollowupQueues()
  State file: /tmp/openclaw-persist-proof-SdQ5bE/live-chat-followup-queues.json
  {
    "version": 1,
    "updatedAt": 1778974677687,
    "entries": [
      [
        "agent:main:dm:telegram-proof",
        {
          "items": [
            {
              "prompt": "did the gateway restart survive?",
              "enqueuedAt": 1778974677687,
              "originatingChannel": "telegram",
              "originatingTo": "99999",
              "run": {
                "agentId": "main",
                "agentDir": "/tmp/agent",
                "sessionId": "sess-proof",
                "sessionFile": "/tmp/sess.jsonl",
                "workspaceDir": "/tmp/ws",
                "provider": "anthropic",
                "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
                "timeoutMs": 30000,
                "blockReplyBreak": "message_end"
              }
            }
          ],
          "mode": "steer",
          "debounceMs": 500,
          "cap": 20,
          "dropPolicy": "summarize",
          "droppedCount": 0,
          "summaryLines": []
        }
      ]
    ]
  }

  Step 2: simulate restart — delete in-memory queue
    FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.get(KEY): undefined

  Step 3: restoreFollowupQueues()
    restored.items.length: 1
    restored.items[0].prompt: did the gateway restart survive?
    restored.items[0].originatingChannel: telegram
    restored.draining: false
    peekRestoredPendingDrainKeys().has(KEY): true

  Pass: queue survives a simulated process restart.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: 1 queued Telegram prompt survived the in-process restart simulation. `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` was cleared to undefined, `restoreFollowupQueues()` re-populated it from the state file on disk, `draining` reset to `false`, and the key was registered in `restoredPendingDrainKeys` so drain scheduling fires when the callback is next registered. State file written and read by `node` against a real temp dir — no mocks, no vitest.
- What was not tested: Full live Telegram→gateway→kill→restart→re-delivery end-to-end (requires a real inbound message arriving during an active agent turn followed by a real process restart).

target.title: docker: support optional pip packages in local builds
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: Local Docker builds support optional apt packages, but there is no equivalent setup/Dockerfile hook for installing Python packages needed by operator-managed scripts or skills. - Solution: Add an opt-in `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES` build arg and pass it through `scripts/docker/setup.sh`. - What changed: Docker builds now install `python3-pip` only when pip packages are requested and `pip3` is missing, then install the requested pip packages with `pip3 install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages`. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Default Docker images and builds are unchanged when `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES` is unset. Existing apt package build args are not changed. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [x] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [x] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Motivation Local Docker builds already support opt-in apt packages for operator-specific runtime needs. Some operator-managed scripts and skills also need Python packages that are not available as part of the default image. This adds a matching opt-in pip package hook while keeping default builds unchanged. - ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Local Docker builds can now install opt-in Python packages through `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES`. - Real environment tested: Local Docker build from this branch using `scripts/docker/setup.sh`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: To run the docker setup with the new parameter: ```bash OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES="requests humanize" ./scripts/docker/setup.sh ``` To test that the requested packages have been installed: ```bash docker run --rm --entrypoint python3 openclaw:local -c 'import requests, humanize; print(requests.__version__); print(humanize.naturalsize(1234567)); print(requests.__file__); print(humanize.__file__)' ``` - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): [1. Terminal Output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27978127/1.Terminal.Output.txt) [2. setup-pip-test.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27978128/2.setup-pip-test.log) [3. import-test.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27978129/3.import-test.log) - Observed result after fix: The resulting `openclaw:local` image includes the requested pip packages and can import them from Python. - What was not tested: Full live gateway startup from this branch was not completed; the setup script reached the image build step, but the temporary-user environment hit a local volume ownership error during the post-build setup phase. The Docker build-arg behavior was verified by importing the requested packages from the resulting image. ## Root Cause (if applicable) N/A. This is an opt-in Docker build customization hook, not a bug fix or regression. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) N/A. No automated regression test added; this is a Docker build customization path and was verified with a real local Docker build. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Operators can opt into installing Python packages in local Docker builds by setting `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES`. Default builds are unchanged when the variable is unset. ## Diagram (if applicable) N/A ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? Yes - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? No - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: When `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES` is explicitly set, Docker builds run `pip3 install` for the requested packages, which may download packages from the configured pip index. This is opt-in build-time behavior only; default builds are unchanged. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux - Runtime/container: Docker local build, `openclaw:local` - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES="requests"` ### Steps 1. clone from the forked repo: ```bash git clone https://github.com/stephenredmond-straiteis/openclaw.git ``` 2. check out the branch: ```bash git checkout docker-pip-packages-build-arg ``` 4. Built the image with: ```bash OPENCLAW_SKIP_ONBOARDING=1 \ OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES="requests humanize" \ ./scripts/docker/setup.sh 2>&1 | tee setup-pip-test.log ``` 5. Ran the Python test for the libraries: ```bash docker run --rm --entrypoint python3 openclaw:local -c 'import requests, humanize; print(requests.__version__); print(humanize.naturalsize(1234567)); print(requests.__file__); print(humanize.__file__)' | tee import-test.log ``` ### Expected - The requested pip package is installed in the built image and importable from Python. ### Actual - both requests and humanize imported successfully and reported version/path. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) [1. Terminal Output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27978127/1.Terminal.Output.txt) [2. setup-pip-test.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27978128/2.setup-pip-test.log) [3. import-test.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27978129/3.import-test.log) # Human Verification (required) • Verified scenarios: Local Docker build with OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES="requests humanize"; verified requests can be imported in the resulting image. • Edge cases checked: Verified python3-pip is installed automatically when pip packages are requested and pip3 is unavailable in the base runtime image. • What you did not verify: Full gateway startup was not tested; the setup script reached the image build step, but the temporary-user environment hit local volume ownership errors during the post-build setup phase. # Review Conversations • [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. • [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. # Compatibility / Migration • Backward compatible? Yes • Config/env changes? Yes • Migration needed? No • If yes, exact upgrade steps: No migration required. Operators who want this behavior can set OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES during local Docker builds. # Risks and Mitigations • Risk: Requested pip packages can add build time and depend on external package availability. • Mitigation: The behavior is opt-in and only runs when OPENCLAW_DOCKER_PIP_PACKAGES is set.

target.title: fix/agents-primary-model-selection-and-catalog-loading-41344
target.body: ## Summary - Fixes incorrect Primary Model selection when switching between agents in Agents > Overview. - Fixes partial / blank Primary Model dropdowns after refresh or first load by ensuring the model catalog is available on Agents Overview paths. - Adds targeted tests for both selection stability and catalog hydration behavior. - Keeps the fix scoped to the Agents Overview model-selection path. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #41344 - Related #46275 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: 1. The agent details subtree could reuse stale DOM/select state when switching agents, which caused the Primary Model select to drift toward the previously viewed agent's state. 2. Agents Overview depended on a shared model catalog that was not guaranteed to be loaded on refresh / first-load / overview-only paths, so the select could render before the catalog was ready and degrade into a partial or blank-looking list. - Missing detection / guardrail: - Existing coverage covered some selected-value behavior, but it did not cover model-catalog hydration timing or the refresh/overview path where the catalog starts empty and arrives later. - Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): - Prior work around #41344 / #46275 addressed the agent-switching selection issue. Revalidating that fix on latest `upstream/main` exposed a second, related lifecycle issue in Agents Overview model-catalog loading. - Why this regressed now: - The Overview UI depends on model-catalog data, but not all entry paths guaranteed that the catalog was loaded before rendering. - If unknown, what was ruled out: - Ruled out a pure selected-state-only explanation. The partial/blank dropdown after refresh was caused by catalog availability/lifecycle, not just DOM selection drift. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should have caught this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.browser.test.ts` - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts` - `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: - Switching between agents keeps the correct explicit Primary Model selected. - The inherit-default option text does not go blank. - When the model catalog is initially empty, Agents Overview shows a loading state instead of a misleading partial list. - When the catalog hydrates later, the full list appears without losing the selected value. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: - The bug spans both UI render behavior and shared model-catalog lifecycle, so a combination of focused controller/unit tests plus browser-level select behavior tests is the smallest reliable coverage. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): - Existing browser coverage covered some selection behavior, but not the catalog hydration path. - If no new test is added, why not: - N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). If none, write `None`. - Switching between agents no longer causes the Primary Model select to show the wrong agent's value. - On refresh / first entry to Agents Overview, the Primary Model select no longer degrades into a misleading partial list. - While the model catalog is still loading, the UI now shows a loading state instead of pretending only a small subset of options exists. - Once the catalog loads, the full option list appears without losing the current selection. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: - N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Windows 10 - Runtime/container: local dev environment - Model/provider: local UI validation only - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): standard Agents UI configuration with default and non-default agents ### Steps 1. Open Agents > Overview > Model Selection > Primary Model. 2. Switch between a default agent and a non-default agent. 3. Refresh the page and reopen the Primary Model select immediately. 4. Optionally wait for model-catalog hydration or switch away/back to Overview. ### Expected - The select reflects the correct explicit value for the current agent. - The inherit-default option text is stable. - Refresh / first-load does not show a misleading partial catalog. - Once the catalog is available, the full model list appears without losing the selected value. ### Actual - Before this fix, switching agents could retain stale select state from another agent. - Before this fix, refresh / first-load could show only fallback options (for example inherit/current) or appear blank until the catalog became available later. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ### UI Evidence #### Before After refresh / first load, the Primary Model dropdown could show only fallback entries or appear blank. #### After After the fix, the Primary Model dropdown restores the full option list correctly after refresh / first load, without losing the selected value. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Switched repeatedly between default and non-default agents and confirmed Primary Model stays tied to the current agent. - Refreshed the page and reopened Agents Overview to verify the select no longer falls into a misleading partial/blank state. - Confirmed catalog hydration restores the full list without losing the selected value. - Edge cases checked: - Inherit-default text path - Explicit non-default model selection - Refresh / first-load / return-to-overview behavior - What you did **not** verify: - Full end-to-end coverage across unrelated panels/modules - Additional stale/error UX beyond this fix's loading-state behavior ## AI Assistance - This PR was developed with AI assistance (CodeX). - I reviewed the resulting changes, reproduced the bug locally, and verified the final behavior manually. - Testing level: fully tested locally with targeted automated tests and manual UI verification. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: - N/A ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: - Revert this PR's two commits. - Files/config to restore: - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.ts` - `ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-overview.ts` - `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-settings.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts` - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: - Primary Model shows another agent's value after switching agents - Refresh / first-load shows only partial model options or a blank-looking select - Catalog hydration changes the list but drops the selected value ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: - Multiple UI entry points now ensure model-catalog availability, which could increase repeated refresh attempts if not properly deduped. - Mitigation: - The loading/ensure logic is centralized in the shared model controller and covered by targeted tests. - Risk: - Keyed subtree remounting can discard unsaved local UI state when switching agents. - Mitigation: - That tradeoff is intentional here because correctness of agent-specific model selection is more important than preserving stale subtree state across agent switches.

target.title: media: fix resource leaks and temp file cleanup in downloadToFile
target.body:
## Summary

- **Fix write stream leak on size-limit abort**: When a download exceeds `MAX_BYTES`, `req.destroy()` was called but the `createWriteStream` was never closed, leaking file descriptors under load
- **Add `res.on('error')` handler**: Response-level errors (premature close, decompression failures) could fire before `pipeline` wired up its own handler, causing unhandled rejections
- **Consume redirect/error response bodies**: Added `res.resume()` on redirect and error status paths to free the underlying socket instead of leaving it hanging
- **Guard against double-settle**: Added a `settled` flag in the Promise executor to prevent resolve/reject from being called multiple times across concurrent error paths
- **Clean up partial `.tmp` files**: `saveMediaSource` now removes the temp file in a `catch` block when `downloadToFile` fails, preventing orphaned partial downloads from accumulating on disk

## Test plan

- [x] Added test: temp file cleanup when download exceeds size limit
- [x] Added test: temp file cleanup on HTTP 500 error status
- [x] All 26 existing `store.test.ts` tests continue to pass
- [x] `pnpm tsgo` — zero type errors
- [x] `pnpm check` — all lint/format checks pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
